topic: Continue CLI Guide: Async Cloud Agents for Developers

key_points: Official Continue blog covers async agent architecture in detail
Explains headless mode vs TUI mode for different workflows
Shows how to use -p flag for non-interactive (scripted) execution
Covers parallel async execution for multiple agent tasks
Official docs: Cloud Agents move AI execution to remote infrastructure
Agents can be triggered manually from a task list or on a schedule
Enables dispatching work instead of just chatting with a model
Covers permission system with ~/.continue/permissions.yaml
Installation via npm: npm i -g @continuedev/cli
Or via curl install script from GitHub
Command: cn (interactive) or cn -p 'prompt' (headless)
Supports --allow, --ask, --exclude flags for tool permissions
Real-world use cases: doc generation, refactoring, code review automation
CI/CD integration: lint, summarize, or refactor on new commits
Custom agents watching for code changes and triggering async jobs
Unix Philosophy-style scripting with headless output
Continue.dev pivoted from IDE extension to async agent platform
Free and open-source — only costs are LLM API fees
CLI-first approach means more setup but agents work in background
Async PR agents enforce team rules without interrupting review
